reason: Exit via user-directed wrap: parameter tuning exhausted productive probes. Synthetic exit criterion far exceeded (n30_clean -78%, requirement -15%). Forest-cat exit criterion only marginally met (+2% paths).
note: Self-improving loop over 10 iters: 17 parameter probes, 3 kept, 14 rejected. Targeted hw_forest_cat quality via 20/24/30 color synthetic oracle.
changes_from_baseline: mergeThreshold: 8 → 4 (narrower ΔE merge window; preserves genuine cluster distinctions)
saliencyWeight: 0 → 1 (new option; biases k-means++ init toward high-chroma pixels)
rejected_probes: denoiseRadius: 2 (at mt=8 +14%; at mt=4 +18%; at mt=4+sal=1 +14%)
autoKStrategy: elbow (+35%, same behavior as silhouette)
saliencyWeight: 1 at mt=8 (+3.5%; order-dependent — became kept at mt=4)
key_insights: mergeThreshold was the dominant single-knob improvement. Default 8 was too aggressive; ΔE=4 is the floor (no cluster pairs closer than 4 in over-clustered output).

Parameter probes are ORDER-DEPENDENT. saliencyWeight failed at mt=8 but succeeded at mt=4 because clean cells had slack to absorb init variance. Re-testing rejected probes after each kept change is necessary.

Clean cells improved dramatically (n24 -82%, n30 -78%); noisy cells barely moved (≤6%). Noise confuses the elbow/merge decisions in ways that denoise or restart count don't fix. A genuine fix for noisy cells likely needs adaptive denoise (noise-detected, not static) or different quantization algorithm entirely.

Forest cat finally shifted at iter 8 (+165 paths, +2%) once the saliency-weighted init restructured cluster picks. Still same 30 unique fills, same color families. Confirms earlier finding: forest_cat tree-door complaint is about color gradation within the brown family, not missing saturated colors — chroma-weighted saliency can't add 'door colors' that aren't there.
autoKStrategy 'elbow' === 'silhouette' in practice (identical outputs across all tests). The three-way knob is effectively two-way.

forest_cat_journey.note: mt change no effect on forest_cat
mt change no effect on forest_cat
FIRST measurable change; +165 paths (+2%) from saliency-weighted init

recommendations_for_next_slice: Salient-color-preserving quantization cannot be achieved with param tuning. Open a new slice that: (a) detects high-chroma spatial outliers in the source image BEFORE k-means, (b) reserves a cluster slot for each high-outlier region.
Noisy-cell improvement requires adaptive denoise: detect estimated σ from input, apply denoiseRadius proportional to it. Global denoise defaults will always trade clean-vs-noisy.

Per-image config: forest_cat specifically benefits from higher k (50 renders 11743 paths vs 8273 at k=30). Ship a per-image override file and a lightweight heuristic (image entropy, edge density) to pick k at ingest.
Silhouette/elbow dispatch into the same code path. Either remove the dead strategy option or fix the dispatch to differentiate them.
